title: Understanding the Problem
text: To find the net force required to stop the car, we need to consider the car's initial kinetic energy and how it is dissipated over the stopping distance.

title: Kinetic Energy Formula
text: Recall that the kinetic energy (KE) of an object is given by the formula $$KE=\frac{1}{2} m v^2$$, where $$m$$ is the mass and v is the velocity.

title: Work-Energy Principle
text: The work done by the net force to stop the car is equal to the initial kinetic energy of the car. This work is given by the formula $$Work=F x$$, where F is the force and $$x$$ is the stopping distance.

title: Equating Work and Kinetic Energy
text: Set the work done by the force equal to the initial kinetic energy: $$F x=\frac{1}{2} m {v_0}^2$$. Solve for F.
